pi_items.source: Consistent across setup_task.sh (line 7) and export_result.sh (line 10); matches task description requirement

Verified against Blender 5.0 Manual (https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/meshes/primitives.html) - all listed as standard Blender primitive mesh types. Task description specifies 'a UV Sphere, a Cube, a Cylinder, a Cone, and a Torus'

Task description states 'Create and assign a distinct material with a unique color to each of the 5 objects' (line 2 of task.json); verified against README.md requirement for 5+ distinct materials (line 32)
Task description states 'Ensure the scene has at least 2 light sources for proper illumination' (line 5 of task.json); README.md specifies '2+ lights' in verification strategy (line 35)
Verified against README.md verification strategy table (line 33): 'Min pairwise distance between mesh objects > 1.5'. Verifier.py line 34 uses same default MIN_SPACING = 1.5

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic Blender scene with 5 standard primitive mesh types (sphere, cube, cylinder, cone, torus) - all verified as valid Blender primitives per official documentation. Requirements are: exactly 5 objects with 5+ distinct materials (colors differing by at least 0.1 in RGB), minimum pairwise spacing of 1.5 units between non-ground objects, a ground plane, and at least 2 light sources. The output file path is /home/ga/BlenderProjects/showcase_scene.blend, validated as consistent across all setup and verification scripts. Data is completely synthetic (procedurally generated by the agent), with no external data sources or downloads involved. Blender file validation checks for 'BLENDER' magic bytes (7 ASCII characters) per official .blend file format specification.
